headline: Retail earnings: What to expect from Kohl's, Dollar General, Dick's
summary: Retailers Kohl's (KSS), Dollar General (DG), and Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) are set to report earnings this week. Forrester Research Retail Analyst Sucharita Kodali joins Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman to discuss the impact of higher gas prices (CL=F, BZ=F) on discretionary spending, trade down to lower-cost retailers, and more. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Target is making 4 big changes to win back customers
summary: Target Corporation (TGT) has had a challenging last couple of years. Several controversies, including boycotts over its Pride collection and the rollback of its DEI (diversity, equity, and inclusion) initiatives, significantly impacted its overall sales. headline: Target's $2B Investment Drives Store Upgrades & Digital Momentum
summary: TGT plans a $2B investment to open more than 30 stores, remodel 130 locations and expand AI tools, aiming to boost growth and enhance its omnichannel strategy.

headline: Target to open 2,000th store
summary: The North Carolina location “represents the future of Target’s elevated guest experience” and is part of the retailer’s plan to open over 30 stores this year.

headline: A Look At Target (TGT) Valuation As Short Term Momentum Contrasts With Longer Term Returns
summary: Why Target (TGT) is on investors’ radar Target (TGT) has been drawing fresh attention after recent share price moves, with the stock showing gains over the past week, month, and past 3 months that stand out against its longer term track record. See our latest analysis for Target. Recent trading has been positive, with a 29.02% 90 day share price return and a 20.18% year to date share price return, contrasting with a 10.96% 1 year total shareholder return and negative total returns over three...

headline: Target Colorado Verdict Highlights Store Safety And Expansion Risk For Investors
summary: A federal jury in Colorado found Target fully liable for a tripping incident that caused long term injuries to a customer. The jury awarded damages to the injured customer, concluding that the hazard was preventable and that Target bore 100% responsibility. The verdict centers on Target's handling of in store safety and its response to a known risk on the premises. For investors watching NYSE:TGT, this verdict puts a spotlight on how a large national retailer manages everyday operational...

headline: Target Gets $145 Price Target (TGT) at Argus with Buy Rating Maintained
summary: Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) is included among the Dividend Kings List: Top 15 Stocks. On March 6, Argus analyst Christopher Graja raised the firm’s price recommendation on Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) to $145 from $125. The firm reiterated a Buy rating on the shares. A few days earlier, on March 3, Target’s new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, told […]
